reviews.review_description:
I have to say, i love the idea of these sorts of games! Laser Dance is really fun, though I hate the heavy AI reliance of the Thief one.
This game really intrigued me, to not just dodge the lasers that come up, but to have a zone that tests you moving around and making it more of a game!
I just tried it today and it does get you moving, thinking, timing and assessing. When I failed it made me want to come back better each time! I like the various courses that come up adapting to your designated play area and it's quite intuitive!
Using hand-tracking instead of controllers for something like this is a very good idea because controllers can make you cumbersome. And this is a step in the right direction! I'm excited to showcase this to family and friends when they come round to make it like a party game!

EDIT REVIEW UPDATE:
Sorry, I thought there was an issue with the health packs on certain levels. But I recently went back to the game and realised what it must have been. It seems different factors played in to it all, that I only had one health left and touching the health pack seemed to finish my run, so I thought it took my last health bar away.
I didn't realise that there were little extra objectives to each level. And this one seemed to be keeping your hands visible at all times. When I tried it again with keeping my hands in front of my face, it worked and I could collect the health pack and finish the level.
Sorry, that's my bad on that. I didn't notice the extra parameter objectives for each level. But yeah, I just got through the campaign and it is great!
The hand-tracking is really good. Though sometimes it does lag a bit but not majorly. I really like what you've done with this! I hope the devs manage to make more. Either levels or another game! And this is a great workout too! After a campaign run I'm out of breath and nearly sweating!

Thank you for the devs for getting back to me. I actually forgot I left a review. Ha. I've changed my score. Sorry again for the mistake

developer_response.body:
Hey, H_Bomb_MB! Thanks for the good and thorough feedback, and sorry for taking so long to come back to you.

Having to replay level 9 after failing level 10, is by design when playing in the "Survival" mode. After every 4th level is complete, there is a checkpoint set so that you will be reset back to the previous checkpoint if failing any of the next 4 levels.

We've sadly not been able to reproduce the health pack bugs you mention though. Do you have any more information you could provide for what you did to make that bug happen? Or could you potentially test the game again and see if it still happens?

reviews.review_description: As an older user of quest I found this app to excellent for getting me active, bending and generally moving around and staying agile. I am playing each day and I highly recommend it for daily activity. It’s also great fun and I enjoy trying to beat the clock.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the feedback, happy to hear it gets you moving!

reviews.review_title: Where Your Room Becomes the Arena
reviews.review_description:
Laser Matrix is an, energetic mixed-reality game that transforms your real room into an interactive challenge. Lasers and targets blend seamlessly into your space, making  you to move, dodge, and react quickly.
The gameplay is easy to pick up but hard to master, focusing on skill, speed, and replayability rather than story.  this is quite an active game

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review and feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Had a great time with my first couple of sessions with this one. That said, I'll give it 4 stars for now (might edit later based on how things go with the game updates) as I almost gave up trying to set up the game for my space as the "auto" room setup was not working well and took me a bit to figure out how to manually configure things for my room setup. 

A tutorial or even instructions on how to grab the handles and set the room would be a massive improvement over the current experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, we will try to address them in future updates. Feel free to follow us on Discord to give feedback directly to the devs.

reviews.review_description:
If you love mixed reality and love dodging lasers, then jump in and have fun.
Slightly different to other laser games as you do not need to have a huge room, you are put into a restricted box where you need to be flexible and bendy.
Good job by the dev.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words and feedback.

reviews.review_title: Great game to make kids move
reviews.review_description: Loved the game myself but loved the fact it made my teen soon have to get up and move more. Good fun and realised I can't bend as much as I used to 😂🤣

developer_response.body: Happy to hear it is bringing the family together.

reviews.review_description: Why this game requires internet in order to even function I have no clue.
This game was a real change of pace for me given how enjoyable it is in a mixed reality setting, and it’s so challenging and moving. kudos to the team!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we appreciate the feedback and kind words.

reviews.review_description: This game is great for anyone whether they've tried VR or not. Get them comfortable with the headset alone and they can play with or without the controllers which I love.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review.

reviews.review_description: I found this version better than Laser dance! Hope to see new level in a Best futur!

developer_response.body: Thanks - Hope you like our new updates.

reviews.review_description:
This game is so good, I like it so much! If you are looking for an exercise game or where you can feel like a secret agent, it's a great choice.

I hope more maps are coming soon, because there are only 16 maps yet. 

It's worth it to buy it for 6.99$

developer_response.body: More maps are coming this month!

reviews.review_description:
This game stands out with gameplay that moves beyond simple laser-dodging!
The diverse mechanics such as moving walls and buttons that change the direction of obstacles really fuel the desire to take on a challenge.
The larger the play area, the more fun the game is to play.
Since it’s still in Early Access, I’m looking forward to future updates and hope that Korean will be included in the added language support.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the kind words! 

Korean and other languages will definitely be added for the full version!

reviews.review_description: Laser Matrix is the kind of VR game that gets you moving without it ever feeling like a workout. Other VR laser games are about flashy visuals or simple sneaking, but this one mixes fitness, fun, and puzzles in a way that feels fresh. It adapts to your space and skill level, so it works whether you are just playing casually or really chasing the burn. Even in Early Access with 16 levels, it already feels polished, and the promise of a campaign and multiplayer makes it even more exciting. If you want a VR fitness game that feels like pure fun, this is it.

developer_response.body: Thank you! A straight to the point mix of fitness, fun, and puzzles is exactly what we were going for :)

reviews.review_description:
I’m very excited about this game and looking forward to seeing it through further development! I first used a playing area of about 10 x 8 which worked very well. Just to see what the difference would be, I  later went to a much larger open area about 25 x 10 and this was even a better experience. 
As for gameplay, I’ve only played about an hour or so in total and I’m up to level 8. It becomes progressively challenging and you have to observe how the laser walls are moving to see the patterns and then make your moves. ￼ Sometimes you have to be quick but other times you have to delay making any movements until just the right moment. So far it hasn’t been too challenging in regards to figuring out how to get to the buttons. I’m assuming that as you progress it will likely become a more difficult puzzle, like a labyrinth with moving walls coming from all sides and above. Luckily there are safety zones that allow you to take stock and figure things out. I highly recommend you try it out! ￼

I bought this game thinking that it will measure out my room and scale by itself, but it seems that it needs a completely empty room to be effective. 
I have to manually adjust the space, which is buggy to grab the handles.
Wish this game would use the cameras to scale the room on it's own to determine the play space
Also some levels require you to go to the ground and crawl, which is a cool feature, but it does not work well, as I keep losing lives because it thinks I hit red zone.
Will give it another shot, but thinking of getting a refund

Update: I figured out the ground crawl, however room scale is still a major issue.

Level 10 - the blue zone moves too fast, was only able to beat the level by setting slow box checkbox. 
It is impossible to get past level 11 with my setup, I adjusted the room as much as I can without furniture in the way, but when I reached level 11, there were 2 issues:
There were targets to hit on each wall, but one wall was all red, and no movement so it was literally impossible to beat. 
The other side, I was barely able to hit the target because the pathway was very narrow.

I feel that this game has potential, but needs major improvement on ability to scale/adjust based on room size

developer_response.body: Thanks for this feedback! This is exactly why we released in Early Access so we could improve issues like this before reaching 1.0. We're looking into improving the room setup and making sure levels are optimized for all room sizes.

reviews.review_description: Instant fun minutes after booting, slick experience and really something different. 10/10 recommend

developer_response.body: 10/10 love this review, so happy you are enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description: One of the best games I have played on vr. Just don't go outside if your dogs made holes in the ground. I snapped my ankle (hurt it) and was forced to carry on. If you can, please make it so you can pause it and have a break.

developer_response.body: Ouch, sorry to hear about the ankle. You can always pause the game by pressing the menu bottom on the left controller! Or the same for handtracking.

reviews.review_description: My gymnast really loves this game. We have an older Oculus and it works fine. We both love the music too.

developer_response.body: That's amazing to hear! Being a gymnast must be a significant advantage :O

reviews.review_description: Loved the way the stages went harder and harder. Can't wait for more levels and multiplayer!
